other_observers: Larry Arnold.; I am the original finder and identifier. I later heard of an earlier sighting of a Red-necked Phalarope on the same pond and same day, but this observer later retracted it and agreed to Red Phalarope. It was also reported seen by Ron Lambeth, Dave Pr
specific_location: It was in a pond directly south of Redlands Parkway, just east of the bridge which crosses the Colorado River. It is known as Redlands Parkway South Pond.
description: Size: swam past hen Mallard which was more than twice its size. Bill black, straight, about as long as the head, heavy at base, blunt tip. Back and wings gray; no white seen in scapulars. Dark primaries. Black ear patch, drooping to rear behind eye. Forehead white. There was a faint reddish wash in upper breast and throat which could be seen only in certain lighting.Behaviors: It was swimming in a small pond. the only other bird on the pond- a hen Mallard. It was actively foraging, gleaning insects from the water surface. It turned one way, then the other, but I didn't see it spinning as phalaropes often do. I did not see the bird fly.Call: none heardPlumage: winter (basic) adult
